intro: Caruelina tristis, commonly known as Desert Lavender or Texas Sage, is a flowering plant species in the Lamiaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, due to its calming effects on the nervous system, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it beneficial in treating skin issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Caruelina tristis include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
